description_long: This is a ceiling-mounted electric fan with four wide, flat blades radiating symmetrically from a central hub. The blades are slightly rounded at the ends and appear to be made of metal or a sturdy composite material. The central hub is cylindrical and connects to a short downrod, which attaches to a circular ceiling mount. There is no visible blade guard, leaving the blades exposed for maximum airflow. The fan has a minimalist, utilitarian design with no visible control panel or decorative elements. The entire object is finished in a uniform, matte gray color.
description: A ceiling-mounted electric fan with four wide, flat blades and a simple, exposed design in matte gray.
description_view_0: The fan is seen from a slightly angled perspective, showing all four blades and the central hub clearly.
description_view_1: This view is similar to view 0 but rotated, with the blades and central hub still fully visible.
description_view_2: The fan appears nearly identical to the previous views, with the same clear view of the blades and hub.
description_view_3: Another rotated view, maintaining visibility of all four blades and the central mounting structure.
attribute_blade_guard_design: There is no blade guard; the blades are fully exposed with no protective mesh or grille.
